label: qwen36-a3b-cpu-post-fdb39b4-fix

system.notes: System under memory pressure during measurement — a separate llama-server instance (~50 GiB resident) holding the same 30 GiB GGUF for an unrelated experiment. dotLLM and llama.cpp runs competed for OS page cache. Numbers represent a floor, not steady-state achievable on a quiescent system. Re-measure on isolated hardware before treating as the authoritative baseline.

verification.dotllm_output: The capital of France is Paris, a city renowned for its iconic landmarks such as the Eiffel Tower, the Louvre Museum
verification.llamacpp_output: The capital of France is Paris, a city renowned for its iconic landmarks such as the Eiffel Tower, the Louvre Museum
verification.match: token-for-token greedy match for all 20 generated tokens

results.note: Hit memory contention during this run

best_warm.interpretation: Using best (lowest-contention) runs: dotLLM 0.8 prefill / 1.0 decode vs llama.cpp 2.86 / 3.92

next_steps: Extend InferenceBenchmarks (BDN harness) to dispatch Qwen3MoeHybrid via Qwen3MoeHybridTransformerModel.LoadFromGguf — currently it only uses base TransformerModel.LoadFromGguf which doesn't handle this arch. Track as follow-up issue.
Re-measure on quiescent system (no concurrent llama-server) for steady-state baseline.
SIMD-vectorise GatedDeltaNetScan.Execute (Vector256<float> for decay/retrieve/update/read inner loops; per Step 63 'Optimize' notes).
Vectorise per-expert MoE matmul in MoeSwiGluMlp where the quantized-direct GEMM is not already vectorised.
